docs.title: ContextGo Docs
docs.description: Official product documentation for installation, agent workflows, automation, remote access, and release operations.
docs.featuredDescription: Use Guides for initial setup, Features for day-to-day product capabilities, and Operations for account, remote access, updates, and troubleshooting.

categories.title: Guides
categories.description: Read these pages before first use. They cover installation, account sign-in, device registration, and the basic product model.

categories.title: Features
categories.description: These pages document the working surfaces in the product, including agents, hooks, scheduled tasks, runtimes, connectors, and skills.

categories.title: Operations
categories.description: These pages cover cloud account behavior, remote access, software updates, release sources, and common troubleshooting steps.

entries.title: Set up ContextGo on your first desktop
entries.summary: Install the desktop app, sign in, register the current device, and confirm that the local host is ready for browser or mobile access.

entries.title: Understand the ContextGo product model before you scale usage
entries.summary: Review the role of the desktop host, cloud account, browser and mobile access, and the release repository before onboarding more devices or teammates.

entries.title: Work with agents, assistants, and runtimes
entries.summary: Use the agent surface to start work, manage assistants as reusable definitions, and keep runtime management separate from task entry.

entries.title: Use harness mode and remote agent collaboration
entries.summary: Understand when work stays on the local desktop, when remote clients can participate, and what to verify before starting a collaborative session.

entries.title: Configure hooks for workflow events
entries.summary: Define when a hook runs, what context it receives, and how to roll out hook behavior without disrupting the main task flow.

entries.title: Run scheduled tasks on a desktop host
entries.summary: Configure recurring workflows, confirm host and runtime requirements, and review failures before relying on unattended execution.

entries.title: Install and manage skills
entries.summary: Review the source of each skill, understand what it adds to the product, and keep installation, updates, and removal predictable.

entries.title: Manage local runtimes and CLI status
entries.summary: Install supported runtimes, repair broken CLI environments, and distinguish between installed, authenticated, and ready states.

entries.title: Use connectors and channels
entries.summary: Connect external sources to the desktop host, route outputs to delivery channels, and document the access requirements for each integration.

entries.title: Use remote access from the browser or mobile
entries.summary: Confirm host availability, understand the device-to-cloud connection, and know what the remote client can and cannot do without the desktop host.

entries.title: Manage cloud account and device binding
entries.summary: Use the cloud account for sign-in, device registration, and lightweight account-linked state without treating it as a full cloud execution environment.

entries.title: Check updates and troubleshoot common failures
entries.summary: Keep the website, the in-app updater, and the release repository aligned, then diagnose login, device, runtime, and update problems from one place.
